purpose: Track how each generated Python command executes implementation-independent command/operation contracts while keeping unavoidable live package behavior explicit.

status_meanings.portable-codegen-primitive-executed: Generated Python command loads a generated operation artifact whose steps are implemented by codegen-owned portable primitives only.
status_meanings.domain-runtime-primitive-via-ir: Generated Python command loads a generated operation artifact and executes it through codegen-owned dataflow, but one or more steps are package-domain runtime primitives rather than portable codegen primitives.
status_meanings.accepted-hand-owned-runtime-primitive: Generated Python parser/dispatch/interface metadata selects an operation id, then a thin _run_*_adapter delegates to hand-owned package runtime primitives. Each accepted entry must declare runtime_boundary_class and runtime_boundary_reason so full completion can distinguish package-specific judgment/live inspection/mutation orchestration from generic deterministic runtime debt.
status_meanings.compatibility-runtime-handler: Temporary unaccepted fallback status; full Python completion must reject this status.
status_meanings.runtime_boundary_class: Classifies accepted hand-owned runtime entries as package-specific-judgment, live-workspace-inspection, mutation-orchestration, front-door-dispatch, provider-integration, or generic-deterministic-runtime-debt. generic-deterministic-runtime-debt blocks full-generated-cli-complete.

completion_rule: Python completion can be claimed only when every generated Python operation is inventoried as portable-codegen-primitive-executed, domain-runtime-primitive-via-ir, or accepted-hand-owned-runtime-primitive; domain-runtime-primitive-via-ir and accepted-hand-owned-runtime-primitive entries are transitional unless their audit proves they do not hide generic deterministic runtime debt.
